contaminant.name: Corrosion Inhibitor Coating
contaminant.description: Corrosion-inhibitor contamination arises from chemical coatings applied to protect metals during storage and shipping, forming a thin, waxy residue that migrates to surfaces over time. This oily layer stands out from rust or dirt by its slippery texture and faint chemical odor, resisting water-based cleaners while adhering unevenly—firmly on steel yet flaking off aluminum and copper more readily. Removal poses challenges like incomplete stripping without damaging substrates, but laser cleaning tackles this effectively by vaporizing the inhibitor precisely, achieving a clean finish that backs up long-term surface integrity.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide, Formaldehyde - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Acetaldehyde - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
